skill.name: vercel-react-best-practices
skill.description: Guides AI agents through React and Next.js performance best practices, including async data fetching, bundle reduction, rendering efficiency, and client-side state patterns.
skill.summary: Apply Vercel-style React and Next.js performance patterns during coding, review, and refactoring.
skill.author: am-will

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many script, command, network, storage, and weak-crypto patterns, but reviewed context shows they are Markdown examples, file names, headings, or reference links rather than executable skill code. No prompt injection text, malicious intent, credential exfiltration, or runnable source files were found. Residual risk is low because some guidance includes browser storage and inline script patterns that users should adapt carefully.

low_findings.title: Documentation-Only Dynamic Imports
low_findings.description: The static script findings point to React and Next.js examples that demonstrate dynamic imports for lazy loading. These are inside Markdown code fences and are not executed by the skill package.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are clearly fenced TSX examples in documentation. No executable JavaScript or package script is present in the repository.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are caused by Markdown code fences, inline code spans, and README command examples such as package manager commands. They do not create command execution by the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sampled lines are documentation syntax or explicit user-run development commands. There are no shell scripts or runtime command invocations in the skill files.

low_findings.title: Reference Links and Fetch Examples Flagged as Network Activity
low_findings.description: The network findings are reference URLs and illustrative fetch examples for React and Next.js data fetching. No evidence shows the skill sends requests, collects data, or exfiltrates information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are public documentation references, and the fetch usage is a small teaching example. No executable network client or destination for collected secrets was found.

low_findings.title: Browser Storage and Inline Script Guidance Requires Review
low_findings.description: Some examples discuss localStorage, cookies, and a hardcoded inline script to avoid hydration flicker. This is not malicious, but copying the pattern into user-controlled content would require sanitization and security review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is legitimate performance guidance, but storage, cookies, logging, and inline scripts are security-sensitive when adapted to real applications.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Obfuscation Alerts Are Scanner Artifacts
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and dangerous-combination alerts appear to be caused by words such as md file names, Markdown headings, reference links, and code examples rather than crypto APIs or obfuscated executable payloads.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Sampled evidence showed documentation text and method names, including .with(), not cryptographic routines or obfuscated execution chains.

dangerous_patterns.title: Inline Script Example in Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes a hardcoded inline script example for hydration timing. It is documentation-only and has no user-controlled interpolation, but downstream users should not adapt it with unsanitized values.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The static pattern is real but appears in an explanatory Markdown example. Risk depends on how a developer copies and modifies the guidance.

content.value_statement: React and Next.js apps can become slow through data waterfalls, large bundles, and repeated re-renders. This skill gives AI agents concrete review and refactoring rules for faster, cleaner frontend code.
actual_capabilities: Identifies sequential awaits and suggests parallel data fetching patterns.
Recommends bundle size improvements such as direct imports and dynamic loading.
Guides Server Component composition to reduce rendering waterfalls.
Suggests client data fetching patterns with SWR and shared subscriptions.
Reviews React state, effect, and rendering patterns for avoidable re-renders.
Provides rule-based examples for React, Next.js, and JavaScript performance work.
limitations: Does not run benchmarks or measure real application performance by itself.
Does not replace framework documentation for version-specific API behavior.
Examples require developer review before use in security-sensitive code.
Focuses on React and Next.js, not general backend performance tuning.

use_cases.title: Review a Next.js Pull Request
use_cases.description: Find data fetching waterfalls, oversized imports, and Server Component patterns that may slow production pages.

use_cases.title: Refactor a Slow React Screen
use_cases.description: Apply targeted changes for re-render reduction, lazy loading, and browser rendering efficiency.

use_cases.title: Generate Performance-Aware Components
use_cases.description: Create new components that follow proven async, state, and bundle practices from the start.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Component Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one React component.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this React component for performance issues. Focus on unnecessary re-renders, effect dependencies, and expensive work during render.

prompt_templates.title: Next.js Data Fetch Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of a route or page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Next.js route for data fetching waterfalls. Suggest parallel fetching, Suspense boundaries, or component composition changes where they fit.

prompt_templates.title: Bundle Size Refactor
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of imports and lazy loading.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these imports and components for bundle size problems. Recommend direct imports, dynamic imports, or intent-based preloading where appropriate.

prompt_templates.title: Full Performance Pass
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a full React and Next.js performance pass on this feature. Prioritize critical waterfalls and bundle issues before smaller rendering or JavaScript optimizations.

output_examples.input: A page awaits user data before rendering the header, sidebar, and main content.
output: The response identifies a server-side waterfall.
It recommends moving independent sections into separate async components.
It suggests Suspense for the data-dependent region when layout can stream first.

output_examples.input: A component imports many icons from a package barrel and renders a heavy editor immediately.
output: The response flags the barrel import as a bundle risk.
It recommends direct icon imports or configured package import optimization.
It suggests lazy loading the editor when it is not needed at first paint.

output_examples.input: A hook reads localStorage on every render and registers repeated global listeners.
output: The response recommends caching synchronous storage reads carefully.
It suggests sharing global listeners across component instances.
It notes that browser-only APIs need server rendering safeguards.

best_practices: Start with critical async and bundle issues before smaller JavaScript micro-optimizations.
Verify each recommendation against the project framework version and existing design patterns.
Keep examples security-aware when adapting browser storage, cookies, logging, or inline scripts.
anti_patterns: Applying every rule mechanically without checking user experience or layout tradeoffs.
Using dynamic imports for small components where complexity outweighs the gain.
Copying example code into sensitive paths without reviewing data exposure and sanitization.

faq.question: Does this skill change code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance for an AI agent or developer to apply during coding and review.

faq.question: Is it only for Next.js applications?
faq.answer: No. Many React rules apply broadly, but several server and routing rules are specific to Next.js.

faq.question: Can it measure performance improvements?
faq.answer: No. It suggests likely improvements, but teams should validate results with profiling and production metrics.

faq.question: Does it require Vercel hosting?
faq.answer: No. The practices are useful outside Vercel, though some guidance references Vercel and Next.js features.

faq.question: Is the guidance safe for security-sensitive code?
faq.answer: It should be reviewed before use in sensitive code, especially examples involving cookies, storage, logging, or inline scripts.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
